title: feat(switchyard): hardening pass post-Q3 incident
author: ravi
head: feat/switchyard-hardening
body: Hardening for Switchyard: structured retries, dead-letter queue, latency p95 instrumentation. Closes #28. Refs PYL-... (no specific ticket; this was the Q3 incident response action item).

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: The retry-budget config is hardcoded; let's parameterize via env so we can tune per-environment without redeploy.

review_comments.author: ravi
review_comments.body: Good call. Pulling out PYLON_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS + PYLON_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE_MS.

review_comments.author: jordan
review_comments.body: Approving. Two follow-up tickets filed for the latency instrumentation breakouts I'd like to see (#31 and the Datadog dashboard work).

title: fix(switchyard): re-register webhooks on GitHub App reinstall
author: jordan
head: fix/PYL-300-app-reinstall
body:
Production fix for #44 / PYL-300. Webhook subscription was only registering on installation.created, not installation_repositories.added (the reinstall path). Adding handling for both events.

Backfill plan: 11 affected customer orgs identified via missing-webhook-events query; will run backfill once this merges.

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: Test coverage on the new event branch?

review_comments.author: jordan
review_comments.body: Added in the test-only commit. Both events covered.

title: feat(switchyard): GitLab webhook ingest
author: jordan
head: feat/PYL-206-gitlab-ingest
body:
Implements PYL-206. Adds /webhooks/gitlab endpoint, HMAC-SHA256 verification, normalization to internal WebhookEvent shape via the Switchyard adapter.

Design trade-off: keeping the GitLab and GitHub adapters as separate modules (rather than a unified abstraction). Rationale in the design doc (#56). Per-vendor logic ends up small and the dispatch is clear.

review_comments.author: ravi
review_comments.body: Nit: GitLab's webhook signature header is `X-Gitlab-Token`, not `X-Hub-Signature` like GitHub. Easy to miss. Make sure we're calling out the difference clearly somewhere people will look in 6 months.

review_comments.author: jordan
review_comments.body: Added a docstring callout in webhook.py + a docs/architecture.md note. Good catch.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Requested-changes on the Beacon side: the normalized event omits the GitLab project_id field which we'll need later for per-project rule scoping. Adding it now is cheaper than adding it later.

review_comments.author: jordan
review_comments.body: Added project_id to the normalized event + tests. Wei, ready for re-review.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Approved.

title: feat(switchyard): GitLab MR comment poster
author: jordan
head: feat/PYL-207-gitlab-comments
body: PYL-207. Posts Pylon's findings as MR comments via GitLab's API. In-line for line-specific findings, summary at MR top for non-line findings.

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: GitLab's discussion / note model is different enough that I want a line-level comment in the code explaining the mapping. Future readers will appreciate.

review_comments.author: jordan
review_comments.body: Added a 12-line docstring on the post_mr_note helper explaining the mental model. PTAL.

title: feat(switchyard): GitLab OAuth + token refresh
author: ravi
head: feat/PYL-208-gitlab-oauth
body: PYL-208. GitLab Cloud OAuth integration. Token + refresh persisted (encrypted at rest). Auto-refresh 10min before expiry. Sentry alert on persistent refresh failure (3+ in a row). Customer-facing 'reconnect' banner if we permanently lose access.

review_comments.author: jordan
review_comments.body: Make sure the refresh-failure alert is loud — silent refresh failure means the customer's analyzer goes dark and they won't notice for hours.

review_comments.author: ravi
review_comments.body: Sentry alert + customer banner both wired. Tested by manually invalidating a refresh token in staging.

title: feat(infra): audit log event schema + ingest
author: ravi
head: feat/PYL-217-audit-schema
body:
PYL-217. audit_log table (append-only via Postgres role permissions, not just convention). Ingest function emit_audit_event() callable from any service. PII sanitization for rule diff content.

Refs design discussion in #88.

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: Postgres-level append-only is the right call. App-role can't UPDATE/DELETE; only DBA role can mutate. SOC 2 will love this.

review_comments.author: tomas
review_comments.body: Sanitization regex review: looks correct. One thing — the rule ID itself is kept raw, which is fine for PII (rule IDs are never sensitive) but worth a doc-comment so future readers don't 'fix' it.

title: fix(tower): cycle-time chart shows actual time for self-reviews
author: camille
head: fix/PYL-301-cycle-time-self-review
body:
PYL-301. Cycle-time was excluding 'self-reviews' (PR author == merger) which made the chart show 0h for repos with single-developer history. The exclusion was added on a hunch and never validated; removing.

Recomputed last 90 days for affected customers.

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: LGTM. Recomputation backfill plan?

review_comments.author: camille
review_comments.body: Backfill script in #36; running once this merges.

title: chore(deps): FastAPI 0.110 → 0.115
author: ravi
head: chore/fastapi-0.115
body: PYL-400. FastAPI bump. One Pydantic schema needed `model_validator` rename (was using deprecated `validator`). Tests passing.

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: Approved. Self-merge fine for deps bumps.

title: feat(beacon): marketplace data model + Postgres schema
author: hugo
head: feat/PYL-200-marketplace-schema
body:
PYL-200. marketplace_rules, marketplace_rule_versions, marketplace_subscriptions tables. JSONB rule content. Soft delete. Semver enforcement.

Design discussion in #112.

review_comments.author: ravi
review_comments.body: Migration runs in ~12s on staging with empty tables; should be fine in prod. Including a pre-migration row count check just in case.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Index on (rule_id, version) — make sure it's a non-unique covering index. Subscribers will query by rule_id and pull all versions.

title: feat(dashboard): audit log UI for Business+ tier
author: camille
head: feat/PYL-218-audit-log-ui
body:
PYL-218. /settings/audit-log route, gated to Business+. Filters by event type, actor, date. Pagination cursor-based. Diff inline if <5 lines, click-to-expand otherwise.

BREAKING: Finding.severity enum normalized in this same release. See migration guide.

review_comments.author: kai
review_comments.body: The expanded diff view jitters on first render — looks like the height isn't reserved before content loads. Layout shift will annoy keyboard users.

review_comments.author: camille
review_comments.body: Reserved min-height on the diff container. Fixed in latest.

review_comments.author: tomas
review_comments.body: Approving. Nice work on the cursor pagination.

title: fix(rules): scope python.bare-except out of test paths
author: hugo
head: fix/PYL-302-bare-except-tests
body:
PYL-302. Arcola Studios reported false positives. Default scoping now skips tests/, conftest.py, test_*.py, *_test.py. Customers can override via config.

Also added the _is_test_path helper to be reused across all rules — print-in-prod, todo-without-author, bare-mutex, async-without-await all use it.

title: feat(beacon): Beacon Rule Marketplace public preview
author: wei
head: feat/marketplace-public-preview
body:
PYL-100. Beacon Rule Marketplace public preview launch. Aggregates several merged-feature branches (PYL-200 through PYL-205).

Launch coordinated with Marketing (PYL-205) — blog post drafted, HN post timed for 9am PT, customer email scheduled. 23 published rules in first 4 days exceeds our internal 10-rule launch target.

review_comments.author: marcus
review_comments.body: Final review pass. Looks great. Tagging v2.4.0 on merge.

title: feat(tower): team comparison data API
author: camille
head: feat/PYL-213-team-compare-api
body:
PYL-213. /api/v1/tower/team-comparison endpoint. Multi-team query against denormalized ClickHouse read model. Permissions filtering at query layer.

p95 466ms for 50-team / 90-day window in staging. Within target.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Permissions check at query layer is correct, but worth double-checking that the user's effective groups are resolved fresh from JWT claims, not from a cache that might be stale during the user's session.

title: fix(dashboard): audit log query streaming for >10k rows
author: tomas
head: fix/PYL-303-audit-streaming
body: PYL-303. Stratosphere Robotics 30s timeout on 12-month filter (~150k rows). Added (org_id, event_type, created_at) covering index. Page-query window capped at 30 days; longer windows go through the export pipeline (background job).

review_comments.author: ravi
review_comments.body: Per-org event-type indexes will help most customers; let's also watch the index size growth — for orgs with 7y retention this gets significant.

title: feat(switchyard): wire GitLab MR diffs into Beacon analyzer
author: jordan
head: feat/PYL-209-gitlab-analyzer
body: PYL-209. GitLab MR open/update events now trigger the Beacon analyzer pipeline. p95 analyzer latency 78s on staging.

title: feat(switchyard): GitLab merge-block enforcer
author: jordan
head: feat/PYL-210-merge-block
body: PYL-210. Pylon registered as required status check on GitLab MRs. Customer-configurable: required vs informational. Override events logged in audit_log per Tomás's review feedback (#168).

review_comments.author: tomas
review_comments.body: Audit-log integration on overrides looks great. Approved.

title: feat(beacon): first 10 async-correctness rules
author: hugo
head: feat/PYL-222-async-rules-batch-1
body:
PYL-222. First 10 rules from the async-correctness rule pack: forgotten await, sync I/O in async, requests in async, blocking sleep, asyncio.gather exception handling, asyncio.create_task reference loss, etc. Each rule has 40+ test examples (20 TP / 20 FP).

Shadow-mode FP rate aggregate: 6.4% across 30 days of customer PR data. Below 8% threshold; promoting to default-on.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Excellent corpus quality. The TP examples for asyncio.gather-without-return-exceptions are particularly good — those are real customer bugs we've seen.

title: feat(dashboard): tower team-comparison dashboard UI
author: tomas
head: feat/PYL-214-team-compare-ui
body:
PYL-214. Side-by-side dashboard for 2-4 teams. Three charts color-coded per team. Absolute / relative-delta toggle.

Design by Felix; backed by API from PYL-213.

review_comments.author: felix
review_comments.body: Loving the relative-delta toggle placement. Felt natural in usability test.

review_comments.author: kai
review_comments.body: Approved. The dense layout works at 4 teams; confirmed in QA.

title: fix(dashboard): marketplace publishing form on Safari
author: felix
head: fix/PYL-304-safari-publish-form
body: PYL-304. TanStack Form interaction with Safari's input event was clearing pasted content on first keystroke. Worked around with a controlled-input rewrite.

title: fix(switchyard): GitLab MR comment idempotency
author: jordan
head: fix/PYL-305-gitlab-dup-comments
body: PYL-305 fix. Verdant Energy pilot reported duplicate Pylon comments on MR retry. GitLab Notes API doesn't have GitHub Reviews' built-in idempotency; we now fetch existing notes and dedupe by stable hash (rule_id + file + line + commit_sha) before posting.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Hash includes commit_sha — good. Means a re-analyze on a new commit doesn't dedupe-out comments that should reflect the new state.

review_comments.author: ravi
review_comments.body: Approved. Worth adding a metric for 'idempotency hit count' so we can monitor how often this fires.

title: fix(switchyard): auto-rotate GitHub App signing key via JWKS
author: ravi
head: fix/PYL-306-jwks-auto-rotate
body:
PYL-306. Production fix. GitHub rotated their App signing key per 90-day policy; we had hardcoded the previous key, causing ~40% webhook failure for ~90 minutes on 2026-04-22.

Now fetches the current key set from GitHub's JWKS endpoint on a 12h refresh cycle, falls back to cached key on transient fetch failures.

review_comments.author: priya
review_comments.body: Postmortem doc filed: docs/postmortems/2026-04-22-github-jwks.md. The 'we hardcoded a rotating key' framing is in there. Approved this fix.

title: feat(beacon): per-rule fire rate visibility
author: tomas
head: feat/PYL-224-rule-fire-rate
body: PYL-224. Customer ask (Stratosphere). Rules listing now shows fire rate per rule (last 30 days). Sortable. 'Tune this rule' link if FR > 15%.

title: feat(switchyard): customizable status check name
author: jordan
head: feat/PYL-225-status-check-name
body: PYL-225. Customers (Kaltsworth Logistics + 3 others) can now customize the Pylon status check name. Default 'Pylon'.

title: chore(infra): pgvector 0.5 → 0.7
author: ravi
head: chore/pgvector-0.7
body: PYL-401. pgvector minor bump. Used for Beacon similarity search (rule deduplication).

title: chore(security): quarterly secrets rotation
author: ravi
head: chore/q1-2026-secrets-rotation
body: PYL-402. Standard quarterly rotation: AWS service-account keys, Datadog, Sentry, Postgres replication, internal HMAC. SOC 2 evidence updated. No service interruption.

title: test(rules): expand bare-except + print-in-prod corpus
author: wei
head: test/expand-rule-corpus
body: Adding 30 more test cases each for bare-except and print-in-prod rules. Pulled from real customer PRs (anonymized). Closes a small calibration gap.

title: docs(architecture): document Switchyard adapter pattern
author: esther
head: docs/switchyard-adapter-pattern
body: Documenting the Switchyard adapter pattern (one module per vendor, normalized event shape). Cross-referenced from PYL-206 design doc.

title: feat(beacon): marketplace rule subscription flow
author: tomas
head: feat/PYL-202-subscribe
body: PYL-202. Subscribe button + integration mode selector. Subscription persisted, audit log entry, customer's pylon.yaml auto-updated via PR.

review_comments.author: tom
review_comments.body: From CS perspective: please make sure the subscription confirmation email links to the rule's docs page. Subscribers always ask 'wait, what does this rule do?' a week later.

review_comments.author: tomas
review_comments.body: Email template links to rule docs ✅

title: feat(beacon): marketplace rule version semver auto-update
author: hugo
head: feat/PYL-203-rule-versioning
body: PYL-203. Minor/patch auto-applies. Major requires manual confirmation. Customer can pin to specific version. Changelog rendered on rule detail page.

title: feat(dashboard): marketplace browse UI
author: tomas
head: feat/PYL-204-marketplace-browse
body: PYL-204. Browse-and-search UI for marketplace. Filters (language, category, publisher, license), search, sort, detail page per rule.

review_comments.author: felix
review_comments.body: Approved. The dense 5-column grid feels right at this scale.

title: WIP: feat(beacon): permissions for tower team comparison
author: camille
head: feat/PYL-215-team-compare-permissions
body:
PYL-215. SAML group resolution + native team-membership for non-SAML orgs. Graceful degradation if user loses access mid-session.

Open review feedback: SAML group resolution caching strategy is being iterated. Will update when settled.

review_comments.author: wei
review_comments.body: Let's pull SAML group resolution out of this PR into a separate component — the auth surface in this PR is getting wide. Same change in code; cleaner review.

review_comments.author: camille
review_comments.body: Splitting now. Will close this and open two narrower PRs.
